commit.message:
test(capture): harden transcript tailer watcher tests

Summary:
- create the watcher smoke-test file before watcher startup and wait briefly for subscription readiness
- make temporary workspaces unique under parallel test execution with an atomic counter

Rationale:
- remove Linux CI flake caused by racing directory creation and file creation after watcher startup
- prevent parallel tests from colliding on the same temp path

Tests:
- cargo test -p claude-insight-capture -- transcript
- cargo clippy -p claude-insight-capture -- -D warnings
- cargo build
- cargo test
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings
- cargo fmt --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(cli): add trace search gc normalize commands

Summary:
- implement storage-backed CLI flows for trace, search, gc,
  normalize, and daemon start/stop with colored terminal output
- add storage helpers for recent session listing, raw-event GC,
  normalization, and FTS rebuild safety
- add CLI integration tests and storage unit tests for the new
  command surface and behavior

Rationale:
- the CLI crate was still stubbed and did not satisfy MOT-124's
  command and help-text acceptance criteria
- storage needed lightweight query and maintenance APIs so the CLI
  could provide real behavior without depending on future TUI work
- renaming the package to `cli` aligns the workspace with the
  ticket's required `cargo test -p cli` validation command

Tests:
- cargo test -p cli
- cargo clippy -p cli -- -D warnings
- cargo build
- cargo test
- cargo fmt --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

test(types): expand fixture and storage coverage

Summary:
- add explicit fixture-backed tests for all hook fixtures and transcript
  entry types covered by the CI-2 corpus
- add forward-compat coverage for ignored unknown fields and unknown
  hook/transcript event handling
- add storage retention GC support plus tests for CRUD, FTS indexing,
  trigger behavior, and old-row deletion

Rationale:
- the ticket acceptance criteria required explicit fixture coverage rather
  than only aggregate loop-based assertions
- unknown hook events and retention GC were not yet implemented, so the
  tests needed minimal production support to validate the requested
  behavior
- the FTS delete trigger keeps SQLite search behavior aligned with raw
  event retention cleanup

Tests:
- cargo test -p types -p storage -- --nocapture
- cargo clippy -p types -p storage -- -D warnings
- cargo build
- cargo test
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings
- cargo fmt --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(capture): tail transcript JSONL files

Summary:
- add a TranscriptTailer that watches ~/.claude/projects recursively
- ingest appended transcript JSONL lines into raw_events with offset persistence
- cover malformed lines, subagent files, resume behavior, and watcher delivery in tests

Rationale:
- capture transcripts directly from Claude session files without backfilling existing history
- persist offsets across restarts so ingestion resumes safely and avoids duplicates

Tests:
- cargo test -p claude-insight-capture -- transcript
- cargo clippy -p claude-insight-capture -- -D warnings
- cargo build
- cargo test
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings
- cargo fmt --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(storage): add incremental raw event normalizer

Summary:
- add a storage normalizer that incrementally materializes sessions,
  prompts, tool invocations, permission decisions, and instruction
  loads from raw_events
- persist a normalization watermark and use FK-safe upserts so reruns
  do not duplicate rows and partial tool/session lifecycles still land
- add fixture-backed normalizer tests covering all hook fixtures,
  transcript passthrough, idempotent reruns, and unknown-event skips

Rationale:
- raw-first storage needs an on-demand materialization path before the
  typed tables are useful to the rest of the system
- watermark-based processing keeps normalization cheap on repeated runs
  and avoids duplicating append-only evidence
- the fixture sweep proves the implementation tolerates the full known
  hook/transcript corpus without widening scope into correlation

Tests:
- cargo test -p storage -- normalizer
- cargo clippy -p storage -- -D warnings
- cargo build
- cargo test
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings
- cargo fmt --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(capture): add JSONL backlog fallback handling

Summary:
- add backlog writer and processor support in the capture crate
- add concurrent append and backlog-to-database tests
- add the hook fallback shell template and align the capture package name

Rationale:
- ensure hook events can be buffered locally when the daemon is unreachable
- satisfy the ticket's required cargo package validation commands

Tests:
- cargo test -p capture -- backlog
- cargo clippy -p capture -- -D warnings

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

feat(capture): add hook receiver endpoints

Summary:
- replace the capture stub router with POST /hooks and GET /health
- persist validated hook payloads into raw_events via SQLite-backed state
- align the package name with cargo -p capture validation and add route tests

Rationale:
- the capture crate needed a real ingestion surface instead of the placeholder
  POST /events route
- request-scoped database access avoids sharing a non-Send rusqlite
  connection through axum state while keeping persistence correct
- matching the ticket's package name keeps the required validation commands
  accurate and repeatable

Tests:
- cargo test -p capture -- hook_receiver
- cargo clippy -p capture -- -D warnings
- cargo build
- cargo test
- cargo clippy -- -D warnings
- cargo fmt --check

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add adversarial-review skill with resume support for iterative review loop

Replaces the codex:codex-rescue-based review workflow with a dedicated
/adversarial-review skill that wraps codex-companion's task mode. Key
improvements: resumable review sessions via --resume flag, wrapper script
for auto-approved invocations, and prompt template aligned with the codex
plugin's built-in adversarial review.

commit.message:
⚡ Bolt: [performance improvement] Add missing sizes to Image fill components (#1039)

* perf(web): add missing sizes to Next.js fill images

Added explicit `sizes` props to `<Image fill>` components across the web workspace to prevent the default `100vw` behavior. This optimizes image loading, saves significant bandwidth, and improves LCP by allowing browsers to download the correctly sized image from the srcset based on the viewport.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: tune responsive image sizes

---------

commit.message:
fix(deb): remove debian/compat — conflicts with debhelper-compat in Build-Depends (closes #90) (#91)

debhelper compat level is already declared via Build-Depends: debhelper-compat (= 13)
in debian/control. The legacy debian/compat file causes dpkg-buildpackage to abort
with "compat level specified both in debian/compat and via build-dependency".

commit.message:
tty: move pcxt console I/O onto VFS backends

Make normal pcxt console I/O live on the VFS side instead of
routing tty and devfs through mod_core UART exports. The VFS
module now builds its own low-level UART and BIOS console
drivers, ttyS0 stays serial-only, and tty1 becomes the BIOS
console on ia16.

With that split in place, the now-dead UART exports are removed
from mod_core and the pcxt far-call glue. Early boot logging
still stays in core through the dedicated pcxt logger.

Verified:
- ./scripts/build.sh pcxt
- ./scripts/build.sh qemu_arm
- ./scripts/build.sh qemu_rv32
- ./scripts/build.sh xtensa_cc

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: PiPAPo

  risk than refactoring auth or routing behavior

commit.message:
klog: add registered logger slots

Stop hardwiring klog to the UART driver and let targets
register explicit logger sinks instead. This keeps early boot
logging in core while making mirrored output a target policy
choice rather than a special-case mirror hook.

This adds primary and secondary logger slots to klog, updates
the touched targets to register their loggers explicitly, and
introduces a small core-side pcxt logger that duplicates output
to COM1 and the BIOS console during early bring-up.

Verified:
- ./scripts/build.sh pcxt
- ./scripts/build.sh qemu_arm
- ./scripts/build.sh qemu_rv32
- ./scripts/build.sh xtensa_cc

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: PiPAPo
repository.description: A POSIX-like OS for microcontrollers overcomes ambiguity between AI-generated code.

commit.message:
sched: rename common yield hook to sched_switch

Xtensa cannot export sched_yield cleanly because that name
conflicts with lower-level runtime code. Instead of carrying a
port-only alias, rename the common scheduler entrypoint to
sched_switch across the kernel and module boundary.

This updates the direct callers, mod_core export tables, and
the pcxt far-call glue so every target uses the same API name.
It also keeps the xtensa-specific behavior inside sched_switch
without the temporary mod_core workaround.

Verified:
- ./scripts/build.sh pcxt
- ./scripts/build.sh qemu_arm
- ./scripts/build.sh qemu_rv32

commit.message:
feat(frontend): add shared error handling flows

Summary:
- add an ErrorBoundary fallback, shared ErrorDisplay, and API error
  handler coverage for 401/403/404/500/network cases
- wire global React Query 401 handling and wrap the app router with the
  new boundary
- reuse ErrorDisplay on the login page and cover the query client/app
  integration in tests

Rationale:
- unexpected render and API failures were previously unhandled and could
  leave users without recovery guidance
- centralizing 401 handling keeps expired sessions consistent across
  local hooks and global query failures

Tests:
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run lint
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run

commit.message:
Feature/issue 74 remove wrapper (#83)

* Add .deb packaging, OpenResty dependency, and systemd service (clean PR)

* Update README with apt installation and service verification instructions

* Remove wrapper mode, add FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT for LLM proxying (closes #74)

- Delete wrapper.lua, wrapper_access.lua, wrapper_body_filter.lua
- Add FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT env var (openai/anthropic/gemini; unset = plain proxy)
- Add FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_AUTH env var for upstream key injection in access.lua
- Move streaming cutoff logic (replace_streaming_cutoff) to llm_limiter.lua
- Simplify access.lua, body_filter.lua, header_filter.lua, init_worker.lua
- Update nginx.conf.template, entrypoint.sh, env_config.lua
- Update README for new LLM proxy model
- Update/remove wrapper unit tests and feature files

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add unit tests for FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT, upstream auth injection, streaming cutoff

- llm_limiter_spec: add replace_streaming_cutoff scenarios (openai/anthropic/gemini/no-marker)
- access_spec: add upstream auth injection scenarios with FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT and FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_AUTH mocking
- env_config_spec: add scenarios for llm_format and upstream_auth field loading
- Restore coverage lost from wrapper_spec.lua deletion

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Cover remaining replace_streaming_cutoff branches (no-finish-chunk, nil-format, non-string)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add targeted coverage tests for streaming and kill_switch edge cases

Cover 13 previously-uncovered lines across streaming.lua and kill_switch.lua
to fix the coverage regression introduced when wrapper.lua was removed.

streaming.lua (+6 lines):
- is_streaming returns false for empty body
- is_streaming returns false when headers is not a table
- is_streaming returns true when stream field is boolean true
- validate_config returns true for nil config
- validate_config returns error for non-table config
- validate_config returns error when streaming field is not a table

kill_switch.lua (+7 lines):
- validate rejects non-table kill_switches value
- validate rejects empty scope_key
- validate rejects route that is an empty string
- validate rejects non-string expires_at
- check() fallback parses iso8601 when _expires_epoch not pre-set by validate

Non-generated coverage: 91.47% → 91.75% (gate: ≥ 91.64%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: replace FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_AUTH with FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADER_* prefix mechanism

Closes #80. Removes the single FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_AUTH variable and
replaces it with a generic FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADER_<NAME> prefix
convention that supports injecting any number of upstream HTTP headers.

- entrypoint.sh packs all FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADER_* env vars into
  FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADERS_PACKED (newline-separated Name=value pairs)
  with _ → - conversion on the header name suffix
- nginx.conf.template declares FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADERS_PACKED instead
  of FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_AUTH
- access.lua parses the packed string and calls ngx.req.set_header for
  each entry; header injection is no longer gated on FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT
- env_config.lua drops the upstream_auth field
- Tests and feature scenarios updated accordingly; README updated with
  the new env var pattern

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: remove stale wrapper mode references from README

- Quick start "Which mode" callout: replace Wrapper bullet with updated
  reverse proxy description mentioning FAIRVISOR_LLM_FORMAT
- Mermaid LLM proxy diagram: replace bare upstream key comment with
  FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADER_* reference

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: restructure Installation section — Docker first, then apt

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add FAIRVISOR_STRIP_REQUEST_HEADERS to drop client headers before proxying

Closes #82. Adds a comma-separated env var that lists request headers to
strip after rate-limit keying but before upstream header injection.

- access.lua: parse FAIRVISOR_STRIP_REQUEST_HEADERS, call
  ngx.req.clear_header for each entry (trim whitespace, skip empty)
- nginx.conf.template: declare env FAIRVISOR_STRIP_REQUEST_HEADERS
- Tests: 4 new scenarios (single header, multi-header, strip+inject
  combo, unset no-op)
- README: document alongside FAIRVISOR_UPSTREAM_HEADER_*

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: expand README for FAIRVISOR_STRIP_REQUEST_HEADERS

- docker run example now shows STRIP + UPSTREAM_HEADER together
- Replace sparse one-liner with a structured "Header handling" section
  explaining both env var families, their naming convention, and the
  order of operations (key → strip → inject → forward)
- Update LLM proxy mermaid diagram to show step 3: strip+inject

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: correct Docker image path to ghcr.io/fairvisor/fairvisor-edge

Two occurrences in README (Docker install example and LLM budget
section) used ghcr.io/fairvisor/edge instead of the canonical
ghcr.io/fairvisor/fairvisor-edge defined in release.yml.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(skills): add new skills for commit, debug, land, linear, pull, push, and skill-creator

Summary:
- Introduced multiple new skills to enhance agent capabilities, including:
  - `commit`: Create structured git commit messages based on changes and session history.
  - `debug`: Analyze logs to identify issues in execution or failures.
  - `land`: Manage PR conflicts, CI checks, and perform squash merges.
  - `linear`: Execute raw Linear GraphQL operations for issue management.
  - `pull`: Update local branches with the latest changes from origin/main.
  - `push`: Push changes to origin and manage corresponding pull requests.
  - `skill-creator`: Facilitate the creation and improvement of skills with user interaction.

Rationale:
- These skills provide essential functionalities for managing code changes, debugging, and enhancing user interaction with the system, thereby improving overall workflow efficiency.

Tests:
- Each skill has been tested for functionality and integration within the agent framework.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
pcxt: stage init and shell bring-up binaries

Build a minimal ia16 userland path so pcxt can boot beyond the
single hello binary and exercise the normal init to shell flow.
This adds the extra syscall/runtime pieces that push needs,
stages base /etc content into the floppy image, and installs push
as /bin/sh for early bring-up.

Keep the ia16 kernel entry paths flat by explicitly clearing ES
alongside DS in the trap and timer handlers before running kernel
C code. That avoids stale segment state during kernel-side string
or memory operations while we continue toward the larger SS fix.

Verification:
- ./scripts/build.sh pcxt

Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

Summary:
- replace the single request helper with typed get/post/patch/delete API
  client methods and shared ApiError handling
- add TanStack Query default stale time and retry settings plus shared API
  type definitions
- cover the API client and QueryClient behavior with dedicated Vitest
  suites and keep auth mutation compatible with explicit auth headers

Rationale:
- SHA-24 needs a reusable frontend API layer with consistent auth,
  error handling, and cache policy defaults for upcoming endpoints
- locking the contract in tests first reduces regression risk for auth
  flows that already depend on the client

feat(frontend): add authenticated app layout

Summary:
- add shadcn-style avatar and dropdown-menu primitives plus Header and
  AppLayout components for authenticated routes
- nest the protected home route under AppLayout and extend router tests
  for the new auth user dependencies
- add header and app-layout component tests covering rendering and

- authenticated pages need a shared header/navigation shell that reads
  the current user from the auth store
- logout should clear client auth state after Firebase sign-out so the
  protected route flow can redirect consistently
- keeping the layout behavior covered at component and router level
  reduces regressions as more protected pages are added

commit.message:
fix(agent-settings): persist updated avatar on save (#2042)

* Include avatar in InlineAgentEditor submit deps and add unit test for avatar save

### Motivation

- Ensure the editor saves the most recent avatar value even when only the avatar changes by preventing a stale closure in the submit handler.

### Description

- Add `avatar` to the dependency array of the `handleSubmit` `useCallback` in `InlineAgentEditor.tsx` so the latest avatar value is captured when calling `onSave`.
- Add a test-only mock for `EmojiPicker` in `tests/unit/InlineAgentEditor.dom.test.tsx` that injects the emoji `😺` when rendered.
- Add a new unit test `saves the latest avatar when only avatar is changed` to assert the saved config contains the latest avatar value when the save button is clicked.

### Testing

- Ran the unit test file `tests/unit/InlineAgentEditor.dom.test.tsx` which includes the new avatar test, and all tests in that file passed.

* test(agent-settings): trigger emoji mock explicitly in avatar save test

---------

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

Summary:
- normalize `/api/v1/auth` responses to an `AuthUser` before storing
- switch default mocks and success assertions to the flat user payload
- keep compatibility coverage for nested `{ user: ... }` responses

Rationale:
- the ticket background and existing repo models describe a flat user JSON,
  while the earlier frontend implementation assumed a nested payload
- normalizing both shapes removes the reviewer ambiguity without depending on
  one undocumented response wrapper

commit.message:
refactor(core): restructure workspace and decode foundations

Summary:
- add shared internal record, json, and ENOENT helper modules
- extract workspace contracts, env bundle logic, metadata, and
  local backend into dedicated modules behind stable exports
- split docker workspace internals into shared, client, inspect,
  hooks, and materialization modules while keeping the public
  backend entry point stable

Rationale:
- reduce repeated casting and boundary-decoding logic so future
  refactors have a consistent internal foundation
- turn the workspace package into a real facade over smaller
  modules instead of a single god file cluster
- shrink the docker backend's responsibility surface before the
  remaining lifecycle and service orchestration extraction

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core lint
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core test

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Show "market coming soon" message and add i18n entriesJimmy/2026 04 01 15 04 45 (#2027)

* fix(i18n): add market-coming-soon copy for remaining locales

* chore: trigger branch sync 3

* chore: trigger branch sync 4

* chore: trigger branch sync 5

* chore: trigger branch sync 6

* chore: trigger branch sync 7

* test(agent-settings): add coverage for market coming soon menu action

### Motivation
- The CI reported missing patch coverage for the `marketComingSoon` branch in `LocalAgents.tsx`, so a unit test was needed to exercise the `Message.info(...)` path when the market menu item is clicked.

### Description
- Updated `tests/unit/LocalAgents.dom.test.tsx` to mock `Message.info` via `mockMessageInfo` so the code path can be asserted.
- Enhanced the `@arco-design/web-react` mocks to render `Dropdown`'s `droplist` and make `Menu.Item` clickable by wiring `onClickMenuItem` through the mocked `Menu` implementation.
- Added an interaction test that uses `@testing-library/user-event` to click the `settings.agentManagement.installFromMarket` menu item and asserts `Message.info('settings.agentManagement.marketComingSoon')` was called.
- Adjusted the test helper to robustly extract the menu item key string so the mocked click matches the real component behavior.

### Testing
- Ran the focused test with `bun run test tests/unit/LocalAgents.dom.test.tsx` and it passed (`1 file, 4 tests` succeeded).
- Ran the full suite with `bun run test` and observed an unrelated existing failure in `tests/unit/SystemModalContent.dom.test.tsx` (`should toggle DevTools when button is clicked`), which did not stem from these changes.

---------

commit.message:
feat(frontend): add router auth guards

Summary:
- add React Router route definitions for `/login`, `/`, and a 404 page
- implement public/protected auth guards with loading UI and redirect state
- cover guard and router behavior with Vitest and router-aware test utils

Rationale:
- the app needs route-level auth control before additional pages can land
- preserving `location.state.from` keeps the post-login return path available
- test-first coverage reduces regressions as routing expands beyond login

Tests:
- npm run test:run
- npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(frontend): add auth API mutation after Firebase login

Summary:
- add TanStack Query client setup and a shared API client for
  authenticated POST /api/v1/auth requests
- implement auth types, mutation hook, store fields, and login page
  wiring to fetch and persist backend user data after Google sign-in
- cover the auth flow with MSW-backed hook tests and login/store
  updates

Rationale:
- the frontend needs to exchange the Firebase idToken for backend user
  data immediately after login so downstream features can rely on the
  Go auth response
- keeping backend auth user data in Zustand separates Firebase session
  state from app-specific profile data and avoids repeating API logic

Tests:
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run lint
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run build

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(runtime): wire manifest env bundles and docker sidecars

Summary:
- resolve manifest host, static, runtime, and service env bindings and
  surface env bundle metadata through workspace and runtime payloads
- provision per-workspace Docker networks and Postgres sidecars with
  readiness checks, init steps, reuse, and cleanup behavior
- inject explicit env into hooks and Codex launch paths while preserving
  local backend behavior, host-side runtime passthrough, and web mocks

Rationale:
- make the frozen runtime manifest operational for Docker-backed
  workspaces without changing the default backend selection model
- fail early on missing host env and keep workspace and service
  lifecycle deterministic and observable
- avoid regressions in local host-path runs, non-default Docker host
  configurations, and downstream runtime consumers

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api lint
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core lint
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/contracts test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/contracts typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/web build
- pnpm --filter @symphony/web typecheck

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
